title: Intensive Optimization
description: Intensive Optimization boosts FPS using mods like Sodium, Lithium, Starlight, FerriteCore, ModernFix, ImmediatelyFast, C2ME, VMP, More Culling, and Enhanced Block Entities. It’s only for 1.19.4 for now, with more versions coming.
body: The **_Intensive Optimization_** modpack focuses on pushing Minecraft **1.19.4** to its highest possible framerate by combining a carefully selected set of performance‑oriented mods that overhaul rendering, simulation, memory usage, and GPU workload. It uses _Sodium, Lithium, and Starlight_ as the core trio for faster rendering, physics, and lighting; _FerriteCore, ModernFix, and LazyDFU_ to reduce memory pressure and smooth out garbage collection; _ImmediatelyFast, FastAnim, Enhanced Block Entities, and More Culling_ to cut down draw calls and simplify what the GPU has to render; and _C2ME, VMP, and Let Me Despawn_ to improve chunk loading, entity ticking, and world simulation. Additional refinements like_ Exordium, FastQuit, Krypton, and GPUBooster_ help reduce overhead in menus, networking, and overlays. Right now, the modpack is available only for Minecraft **1.19.4**, since many of these optimizations are version‑sensitive and require careful compatibility testing. Support for newer versions is planned, but not ready yet. Users who want to add Iris or Nvidium should do so cautiously, as shader engines and experimental renderers may conflict with the pack’s optimizations and are not officially supported. For even higher FPS, players can experiment with increasing **RAM allocation** (within reasonable limits) and trying different** Java arguments** from trusted optimization communities to find the best setup for your hardware.
